raw_consumer_statement: On or around XX/XX/year>, My American Express Gold card showed a charge of {$1000.00} to a company called XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX  Software. I never made any such purchase for any reason. I had been making minimum payments to American Express, which were around $ XXXX over time on charges I can account for. When I tried to contact this company who charged my card and to dispute the transaction, I was not able to get a representative on the phone to find out why my credit card was fraudulently charged. I also tried contacting American Express, but there were not very cooperative in hearing my inquiry or doing anything about the fraudulent charge. My company eventually went out of business, due to financial hardship, and I was unable to make payments to my credit card and American Express was sending bills with interest charges, late fees and all kind of other charges. On top of that, they cancelled my American XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, which I was paying on time, each month, with a monthly payment of {$40.00}. Because of the issues I was experiencing with my American Express XXXX XXXX, they suspended my usage on my personal AMEX card. Because I was unable to make payments on either card, they closed my account and sent my account to a few collection agencies ( XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX, Portfolio Management ). The amount that American Express is saying that I owe is {$4700.00} ( American Express XXXX XXXX ) and {$1300.00} ( American Express XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ). This now shows on my credit report, which has impacted my XXXX XXXX adversely, making it difficult to restore my creditworthiness going forward.
